title: Integers & Rational Numbers
description: Master integer operations, absolute value, and rational number concepts including addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division of positive and negative numbers.

hints: When you add two negative numbers, you add their absolute values.
The result is always negative when both numbers are negative.
questions.explanation: Adding two negative numbers: add the absolute values (5 + 3 = 8) and keep the negative sign, giving -8.

questions.text: What is the absolute value of -12?
hints: Absolute value measures distance from zero on the number line.
questions.explanation: The absolute value of -12 is 12 because -12 is 12 units away from zero on the number line.

questions.text: The absolute value of any number is always positive or zero.
hints: Think about what absolute value represents on a number line.
Can a distance ever be negative?
questions.explanation: Absolute value represents distance from zero, and distance is always non-negative. So |x| >= 0 for all x.

questions.text: What is (-4) x (-6)?
hints: Multiplying two negative numbers gives what sign?
questions.explanation: A negative times a negative equals a positive: (-4) x (-6) = 24.

questions.text: What is 7 + (-10)?
hints: Adding a negative is the same as subtracting.
questions.explanation: 7 + (-10) = 7 - 10 = -3. Since 10 has a larger absolute value, the result is negative.

questions.text: The product of a positive integer and a negative integer is always negative.
hints: Think about the sign rules for multiplication.
Positive times negative gives what sign?
questions.explanation: When you multiply a positive number by a negative number, the result is always negative.

questions.text: Which fraction is equivalent to 0.75?
questions.explanation: 0.75 = 75/100 = 3/4 after dividing numerator and denominator by 25.

hints: Dividing two negative numbers gives what sign?
questions.explanation: A negative divided by a negative equals a positive: (-18) / (-3) = 6.

questions.text: Every integer is a rational number.
hints: A rational number can be written as a fraction a/b where b is not zero.
Can you write any integer as a fraction?
questions.explanation: Every integer n can be written as n/1, which is a fraction with integer numerator and non-zero integer denominator. So every integer is rational.

hints: First compute the sum inside the absolute value.
Then take the absolute value of the result.
The denominators are the same, so just add the numerators.
questions.explanation: Since the denominators match: (-2 + 1)/3 = -1/3.

questions.text: If the temperature drops from 5°F to -8°F, what is the total change in temperature?
questions.explanation: Change = -8 - 5 = -13°F. The temperature dropped 13 degrees.

hints: Multiply two at a time. (-3) x (-2) first.
An odd number of negative factors gives a negative product.
questions.explanation: (-3) x (-2) = 6, then 6 x (-4) = -24. With three negative factors (odd count), the product is negative.

questions.text: The number -7/4 is located between -2 and -1 on the number line.
questions.explanation: -7/4 = -1.75, and -2 < -1.75 < -1, so it lies between -2 and -1.

hints: Subtracting a negative is the same as adding.

questions.explanation: (-3/5) x (10/9) = -30/45 = -2/3 after dividing numerator and denominator by 15.

questions.text: If a and b are both negative integers, then a - b is always negative.
hints: Try a = -2, b = -5. What is a - b?
questions.explanation: If a = -2 and b = -5, then a - b = -2 - (-5) = -2 + 5 = 3, which is positive. So it is not always negative.

questions.text: A submarine is at -200 feet. It rises 75 feet, then descends 50 feet. What is its final depth?
hints: Rising means adding, descending means subtracting.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a rational number?
hints: A rational number can be written as a ratio of two integers.
Which of these cannot be expressed as a/b?
questions.explanation: sqrt(2) is irrational because it cannot be expressed as a fraction of two integers. The others are all rational.

questions.text: |a - b| is always equal to |b - a|.
hints: a - b and b - a are opposites of each other.
What does absolute value do to opposites?
questions.explanation: a - b = -(b - a), so |a - b| = |-(b - a)| = |b - a|. Absolute value of opposites are equal.

questions.text: If x is an integer and |x| = 7, what is the product of all possible values of x?
hints: If |x| = 7, what are the possible values of x?
questions.explanation: |x| = 7 means x = 7 or x = -7. Their product is 7 x (-7) = -49.

questions.text: The sum of three consecutive integers is -42. What is the largest of the three?
hints: Let the integers be n-1, n, n+1.
questions.explanation: Let the integers be n-1, n, n+1. Then 3n = -42, so n = -14. The largest is n+1 = -13.

questions.text: If a/b = -3/4 and b/c = 2/5, what is a/c?
For all integers a and b, |a + b| <= |a| + |b|.
hints: Try a few examples with positive and negative numbers.
questions.explanation: This is the triangle inequality. When a and b have opposite signs, cancellation makes |a + b| less than |a| + |b|. When same sign, they are equal.

questions.text: A stock price changes by -3, +5, -7, +2, and -1 over five days. What is the average daily change?
hints: Divide the total by the number of days.
questions.explanation: Total change = -3 + 5 + (-7) + 2 + (-1) = -4. Average = -4/5.

title: Pythagorean Theorem & Applications
description: Explore the Pythagorean theorem, identify Pythagorean triples, calculate distances on coordinate planes, and extend to 3D diagonal problems.

questions.text: In a right triangle with legs 3 and 4, what is the length of the hypotenuse?
The Pythagorean theorem only works for right triangles.
hints: Think about what makes the theorem special.
It relates the sides using the right angle.
questions.explanation: The Pythagorean theorem (a^2 + b^2 = c^2) applies only to right triangles, where c is the hypotenuse opposite the right angle.

questions.text: Which set of numbers is a Pythagorean triple?
In a right triangle, the hypotenuse is always the _____ side.
hints: The hypotenuse is opposite the largest angle.
The right angle (90°) is the largest angle in a right triangle.
questions.explanation: The hypotenuse is opposite the right angle (the largest angle), making it always the longest side of a right triangle.

questions.text: A right triangle has legs of length 6 and 8. What is the hypotenuse?
hints: This is a multiple of the 3-4-5 triple.
questions.explanation: 6^2 + 8^2 = 36 + 64 = 100 = 10^2, so the hypotenuse is 10. This is 2 x (3, 4, 5).

questions.text: (6, 8, 10) is a Pythagorean triple.
questions.explanation: 6^2 + 8^2 = 36 + 64 = 100 = 10^2. Yes, (6, 8, 10) is a Pythagorean triple (it's 2 times the 3-4-5 triple).

questions.text: What is the distance between the points (0, 0) and (3, 4)?
hints: Use the distance formula or think of it as a right triangle.
The horizontal leg is 3 and the vertical leg is 4.

questions.text: A triangle with sides 7, 24, and 25 is a right triangle.
questions.explanation: 7^2 + 24^2 = 49 + 576 = 625 = 25^2. Since a^2 + b^2 = c^2, it is a right triangle.

questions.text: If a right triangle has a hypotenuse of 13 and one leg of 5, what is the other leg?
hints: Use a^2 + b^2 = c^2 and solve for the missing leg.

questions.text: The distance formula is derived from which theorem?
hints: The distance formula uses squares and a square root.
It treats the horizontal and vertical distances as legs.
questions.explanation: The distance formula d = sqrt((x2-x1)^2 + (y2-y1)^2) is a direct application of the Pythagorean theorem.

questions.text: A ladder 15 feet long leans against a wall. The base is 9 feet from the wall. How high up the wall does the ladder reach?
hints: The ladder is the hypotenuse, the distance from wall is one leg.

questions.text: What is the distance between points (1, 2) and (4, 6)?
If a triangle has sides 9, 12, and 15, then it is a right triangle.
questions.explanation: 9^2 + 12^2 = 81 + 144 = 225 = 15^2. This is 3 x (3, 4, 5), confirming it's a right triangle.

questions.text: A rectangular field is 40 meters by 30 meters. What is the diagonal distance across the field?
hints: The diagonal forms a right triangle with the sides.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a Pythagorean triple?
hints: Check each: does a^2 + b^2 = c^2?

questions.text: An isosceles right triangle has legs of length 1. What is the length of its hypotenuse?
If a^2 + b^2 > c^2 (where c is the longest side), the triangle is acute.
hints: For a right triangle, a^2 + b^2 = c^2 exactly.
If the sum of squares of the shorter sides exceeds the square of the longest side, the angle is less than 90°.
questions.explanation: When a^2 + b^2 > c^2, the angle opposite side c is less than 90°, making all angles acute.

questions.text: A right triangle has a hypotenuse of 10 and one leg of 6. What is the area of the triangle?
questions.explanation: Missing leg = sqrt(100 - 36) = sqrt(64) = 8. Area = (1/2)(6)(8) = 24.

questions.text: What is the distance between (-2, 3) and (4, -5)?
Every multiple of a Pythagorean triple is also a Pythagorean triple.
hints: If (a, b, c) is a triple, check (ka, kb, kc).

questions.text: A box is 3 feet long, 4 feet wide, and 12 feet tall. What is the length of the space diagonal (corner to opposite corner)?
hints: Use the 3D distance formula: d = sqrt(l^2 + w^2 + h^2).

questions.text: Two ships start at the same port. One sails 8 miles north, then 6 miles east. The other sails 15 miles south. How far apart are the two ships?
hints: Ship 1 is at (6, 8). Ship 2 is at (0, -15).
Find the distance between those two points.

questions.text: The diagonal of a square is 10 cm. What is the side length of the square?
hints: If the side is s, then s^2 + s^2 = 10^2.

questions.text: The 3D distance formula sqrt(a^2 + b^2 + c^2) can be derived by applying the Pythagorean theorem twice.
hints: First find the diagonal of the base: sqrt(a^2 + b^2).
Then use that as one leg and c as the other.
questions.explanation: First, d_base = sqrt(a^2 + b^2). Then d_space = sqrt(d_base^2 + c^2) = sqrt(a^2 + b^2 + c^2). This uses the theorem twice.

questions.text: A right triangle has an area of 30 and one leg of 5. What is the length of the hypotenuse?
hints: Area = (1/2)(5)(other leg) = 30. Find the other leg first.

title: Systems of Equations
description: Solve systems of linear equations using substitution, elimination, and graphical methods. Apply systems to real-world word problems.

questions.text: Solve the system: x + y = 10, x - y = 4. What is x?
hints: The y terms will cancel out.

questions.text: In the system x + y = 10, x - y = 4, what is y?
questions.explanation: x = 7, so 7 + y = 10, giving y = 3.

questions.text: A system of equations can have exactly one solution, no solution, or infinitely many solutions.
hints: Think about two lines on a graph.
They can intersect once, be parallel, or overlap.
questions.explanation: Two lines can intersect at one point (one solution), be parallel (no solution), or be the same line (infinitely many solutions).

questions.text: If y = 2x and x + y = 9, what is x?
hints: Substitute y = 2x into the second equation.
questions.explanation: Substituting: x + 2x = 9 → 3x = 9 → x = 3.

questions.text: Two lines that are parallel have how many solutions as a system?
questions.explanation: Parallel lines never meet, so there are no solutions (the system is inconsistent).

questions.text: The solution to a system of two equations is the point where the two lines intersect on a graph.
hints: The solution satisfies both equations simultaneously.
questions.explanation: The intersection point is the (x, y) pair that satisfies both equations, which is the solution.

questions.text: Solve: y = x + 1 and y = 3. What is x?
hints: Since y = 3, substitute into the first equation.
questions.explanation: Substituting y = 3: 3 = x + 1, so x = 2.

questions.text: Which method involves adding or subtracting equations to eliminate a variable?
hints: This method combines equations to cancel one variable.
It's named after what it does to variables.
questions.explanation: The elimination method involves adding or subtracting equations to eliminate one variable.

questions.text: The system y = 2x + 1 and y = 2x + 5 has no solution.
hints: Both lines have the same slope.
Lines with equal slopes but different y-intercepts are parallel.
questions.explanation: Both lines have slope 2 but different y-intercepts (1 and 5), so they are parallel and never intersect.

questions.text: In the substitution method, the first step is to:

choices: Solve one equation for one variable
Set both equations equal to zero

hints: You need to express one variable in terms of the other.
Then you plug that expression into the other equation.
questions.explanation: In substitution, you first isolate one variable in one equation, then substitute that expression into the other equation.

questions.text: Solve by elimination: 2x + 3y = 12 and 2x - 3y = 0. What is x?
hints: Add the equations to eliminate y.
questions.explanation: Adding: 4x + 0y = 12, so x = 3.

questions.text: A movie ticket costs $8 and popcorn costs $5. Lisa spent $34 buying a total of 5 items. How many movie tickets did she buy?
hints: Let t = tickets and p = popcorn. t + p = 5 and 8t + 5p = 34.

questions.text: Solve: 3x + 2y = 16 and x - y = 2. What is y?
hints: From the second equation, x = y + 2.

questions.text: The system x + y = 5 and 2x + 2y = 10 has infinitely many solutions.
questions.explanation: Dividing the second equation by 2 gives x + y = 5, which is identical to the first. The lines overlap, giving infinitely many solutions.

questions.text: Solve: 4x - y = 10 and 2x + y = 8. What is the solution (x, y)?
hints: Add the equations to eliminate y.
questions.explanation: Adding: 6x = 18, x = 3. Then 2(3) + y = 8, y = 2. Solution: (3, 2).

questions.text: The sum of two numbers is 20 and their difference is 6. What are the two numbers?
questions.explanation: x + y = 20, x - y = 6. Adding: 2x = 26, x = 13. Then y = 7.

questions.text: If two equations have different slopes when written in slope-intercept form, the system has exactly one solution.
hints: Different slopes mean the lines are not parallel.
questions.explanation: Lines with different slopes are not parallel and must intersect at exactly one point, giving exactly one solution.

questions.text: Solve: 5x + 2y = 1 and 3x + 2y = -3. What is x?
hints: Subtract the second equation from the first.

questions.text: A parking lot has motorcycles (2 wheels) and cars (4 wheels). There are 10 vehicles and 28 wheels total. How many cars are there?
You can always use substitution to solve a system of two linear equations.
hints: You can always isolate one variable from one equation.
questions.explanation: Substitution always works for systems of linear equations, though sometimes elimination or graphing may be more efficient.

questions.text: Solve: x + 2y = 7 and 3x - 2y = 5. What is x?
hints: Add the two equations to eliminate y.
questions.explanation: Adding the equations: (x + 2y) + (3x - 2y) = 7 + 5 → 4x = 12 → x = 3. Then 3 + 2y = 7, so y = 2.

questions.text: A boat travels 30 miles upstream in 3 hours and 30 miles downstream in 2 hours. What is the speed of the boat in still water?
hints: Let b = boat speed and c = current speed.
Upstream: b - c = 30/3 = 10. Downstream: b + c = 30/2 = 15.
questions.explanation: b - c = 10 and b + c = 15. Adding: 2b = 25, b = 12.5 mph.

questions.text: For what value of k does the system x + 2y = 5 and 2x + 4y = k have infinitely many solutions?
hints: For infinitely many solutions, the equations must be equivalent.
questions.explanation: Multiplying eq1 by 2: 2x + 4y = 10. For the equations to be identical, k must equal 10.

questions.text: The system 2x + 3y = 7 and 4x + 6y = 15 has no solution.
questions.explanation: Multiplying eq1 by 2 gives 4x + 6y = 14, but eq2 says 4x + 6y = 15. Since 14 ≠ 15, the lines are parallel with no solution.

questions.text: At a school fair, adult tickets cost $6 and child tickets cost $3. If 200 tickets were sold for a total of $900, how many adult tickets were sold?
hints: From the first: c = 200 - a. Substitute.

title: Exponents & Scientific Notation
description: Master exponent rules including product, quotient, and power rules. Work with negative exponents and convert between standard and scientific notation.

questions.text: What is 2^3 x 2^4?
hints: When multiplying same bases, add the exponents.
questions.explanation: When multiplying powers with the same base, add exponents: 2^3 x 2^4 = 2^(3+4) = 2^7.

questions.text: What is (3^2)^3?
hints: Power of a power: multiply exponents.
questions.explanation: When raising a power to a power, multiply exponents: (3^2)^3 = 3^(2x3) = 3^6.

questions.text: Any non-zero number raised to the power of 0 equals 1.
questions.explanation: By definition and pattern, a^0 = 1 for any non-zero value of a.

questions.text: What is 5^6 / 5^2?
hints: When dividing same bases, subtract exponents.
questions.explanation: When dividing powers with the same base, subtract exponents: 5^6 / 5^2 = 5^(6-2) = 5^4.

questions.text: Express 45,000 in scientific notation.
hints: Move the decimal so there's one non-zero digit before it.
Count how many places you moved the decimal.
questions.explanation: 45,000 = 4.5 x 10^4. Move the decimal 4 places left.

questions.text: What is 3^(-2)?
hints: A negative exponent means take the reciprocal.

questions.text: Which of these is written in proper scientific notation?
hints: In scientific notation, the coefficient must be >= 1 and < 10.
Which option has a coefficient between 1 and 10?
questions.explanation: Scientific notation requires a coefficient between 1 and 10. Only 3.7 x 10^5 meets this requirement.

questions.text: What is 0.00056 in scientific notation?
hints: Move the decimal right until you have a number between 1 and 10.
questions.explanation: 0.00056 = 5.6 x 10^(-4). The decimal moves 4 places to the right.

questions.text: What is (4 x 10^3) x (2 x 10^5) in scientific notation?
Simplify: x^5 / x^(-2).
(ab)^n = a^n x b^n for all values of n.
hints: This is the power of a product rule.
questions.explanation: The power of a product rule states that (ab)^n = a^n x b^n. This works for all values of n.

questions.text: The distance from Earth to the Sun is about 1.5 x 10^8 km. The distance from Earth to Mars is about 7.5 x 10^7 km. How many times farther is the Sun than Mars?

hints: A negative exponent flips the fraction.

questions.text: 5.0 x 10^0 equals 5.
Simplify: (3x^2y)^3.
hints: Apply the exponent to each factor.

questions.text: Express 6.02 x 10^23 in words (order of magnitude).
questions.explanation: 6.02 x 10^23 is approximately 602 sextillion (602,000,000,000,000,000,000,000). This is Avogadro's number.

questions.text: x^(-1) is always equal to 1/x for x ≠ 0.
hints: The negative exponent rule says a^(-n) = 1/a^n.
questions.explanation: By definition, x^(-1) = 1/x^1 = 1/x for any non-zero x.

questions.text: Simplify: (2^(-3) x 4^2) / 8. Express as a power of 2.
A computer performs 3.2 x 10^9 operations per second. How many operations does it perform in 5 x 10^2 seconds?
hints: 3.2 x 5 = 16, then adjust to proper scientific notation.

questions.text: If 2^x = 8^3, what is x?
The expression 2^10 is greater than 10^3.
questions.explanation: 2^10 = 1024 and 10^3 = 1000. Since 1024 > 1000, the statement is true.

questions.text: Simplify: (6 x 10^5)^2 / (3 x 10^4). Express in scientific notation.
